title: Tempo of the Rush Hour
description: The Tempo materializes in the cul-de-sac at 8:47 AM, vibrating at a frequency slightly wrong for the suburb. Three minutes behind schedule.

steps.text: The figure appeared at the end of the cul-de-sac moving at a speed that was technically legal but suggested someone who had never in their existence been late and was not going to start now. The briefcase was a blur. The tie was a blur. The expression was acute personal crisis.

lines.speaker: Tempo of the Rush Hour
lines.text: LATE. LATE LATE LATE. You are three minutes behind schedule.

lines.speaker: Tempo of the Rush Hour
lines.text: EVERYONE has a schedule. The schedule does not require acknowledgment to function. The 8:44 arrival window has CLOSED.

lines.speaker: Tempo of the Rush Hour
lines.text: For THIS. For the confrontation. For the thing we are currently doing. You have disrupted the flow. You have caused downstream latency. The 9:15 cannot be recovered.

lines.speaker: Mage
lines.text: There are no other appointments. This suburb is empty.

lines.speaker: Tempo of the Rush Hour
lines.text: The schedule does not care about occupancy. It cares about adherence. Three minutes lost compounds. Three minutes becomes six. Six becomes the entire morning. The entire morning becomes the entire system.

lines.speaker: Tempo of the Rush Hour
lines.text: We will resolve this quickly. I have a 9:30.

lines.speaker: Tempo of the Rush Hour
lines.text: I don't know. It was already on the calendar when I arrived.
